digest.editor_note: Fellow Humans, the Iran war is now doing what wars do to energy markets — it's pushing gas prices up, squeezing small businesses already battered by tariffs, and sending Bitcoin into a short-squeeze whipsaw that briefly looked like recovery. The dominant story today is not any single asset class but the cascade: conflict hits oil, oil hits margins, margins hit Main Street, and leveraged crypto traders get caught in the crossfire.

sentiment.label: Cautious
sentiment.context: Iran war escalation is lifting energy prices and hammering small business margins, while crypto markets show tentative stabilization after a geopolitical-driven flush — net picture is cautious with downside risks dominant.

stories.headline: Iran War Pushes Gas to $3.25/Gallon, Diesel Up 11% in a Week — Small Businesses Squeezed
stories.body: Average U.S. gas prices hit $3.25 a gallon, up 36 cents from last month, while diesel surged 11% week-over-week to $4.16 — a direct consequence of supply disruptions tied to the U.S.-Israel strikes on Iran. Small businesses, already reporting their weakest revenue and hiring expectations since 2020 per a Federal Reserve study released this week, are absorbing the double hit of tariff-driven input costs and now fuel price spikes. For delivery-dependent operators, margin compression is acute and price increases to consumers are increasingly unavoidable.

stories.headline: Prediction Market Trader Made $553K Betting on Khamenei's Ouster — Before the Strike
stories.body: A Polymarket account under the username 'Magamyman' netted over $553,000 betting that Iran's Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei would be out of power — placed just before an Israeli strike killed him on Saturday. The platform, advised by Donald Trump Jr., is now facing pointed questions about whether trades were placed on material non-public information, a legal grey zone prediction markets have so far navigated by sidestepping securities law classifications. Hundreds of millions of dollars were wagered across Iran-related markets in the days preceding the strikes.

stories.headline: Bitcoin Short Squeeze Wipes $272M in Positions as BTC Rebounds Above $73K
stories.body: Bitcoin dropped to the $63,000 zone as Iran-driven geopolitical fear collapsed open interest from ~$29B to ~$21B, flushing leveraged longs. The subsequent stabilization and rebound above $73,000 triggered a sharp short squeeze, with $272M in short positions liquidated and total crypto liquidations hitting $587.97M in 24 hours. Altcoins followed with Solana +9%, Chainlink +7%, and Hyperliquid +12% over seven days — though the Coinbase Premium Index's prior deep negative readings suggest U.S. spot demand remains structurally cautious.

stories.headline: China's 15th Five-Year Plan Bets on Tech Over Consumption Amid Deepening Economic Imbalances
stories.body: Beijing's new quinquennial plan doubles down on technology as the primary growth engine, explicitly deprioritizing domestic consumption despite well-documented structural imbalances in the Chinese economy. The strategy reflects a political calculation that self-sufficiency in advanced technology — driven in part by U.S. export restrictions — matters more than rebalancing household demand. With the U.S.-China tech race now framed as existential by both sides, the plan accelerates a bifurcation of global technology supply chains that carries long-term implications for multinational earnings and trade flows.

stories.headline: Evervault Raises $25M Series B to Encrypt Payment Data at Point of Collection
stories.body: Evervault, backed by Ribbit Capital, Sequoia, and Index Ventures, has raised $25M to scale its payment data encryption platform that ensures sensitive card data never exists in plaintext — on any server, including the merchant's. The company uses AWS Nitro Enclaves and a dual-custody key model via Shamir's Secret Sharing, and claims clients can reduce their PCI DSS compliance scope to its simplest tier, saving roughly $100,000 annually. With payment fraud losses running into the tens of billions globally, the infrastructure-layer approach to eliminating plaintext data exposure is gaining traction with regulated financial institutions.

stories.headline: Colliers Names New Global CFO and CIO as Veteran CEO McLernon Retires After 40 Years
stories.body: Colliers (NASDAQ/TSX: CIGI) has appointed Christian Mayer as Global CFO & CEO of Commercial Real Estate and Elias Mulamoottil as Global CIO & CEO of Engineering, effective immediately, as part of a planned leadership transition. The moves come alongside the pending close of the Ayesa acquisition, which will push Colliers Engineering to over 13,000 professionals across 20+ countries. Long-tenured CEO of Real Estate Services Chris McLernon will retire at end of April after nearly 40 years — a generational handoff at one of the world's largest diversified real estate and infrastructure services firms.

stories.headline: LNG Shipper CCEC Orders Three More Carriers, Accelerates Exit from Container Shipping
stories.body: Capital Clean Energy Carriers Corp. (NASDAQ: CCEC) reported Q4 2025 results highlighting continued execution of its pivot to LNG transportation, with three additional latest-technology LNG carriers ordered for 2028-2029 delivery and the sale of another Neo-Panamax container vessel completed. CEO Jerry Kalogiratos positioned the company as the largest U.S.-listed LNG shipping firm, with contracted cash flows underwriting the orderbook. The Iran conflict and associated energy supply disruptions add a near-term demand tailwind to LNG shipping rates that CCEC's contracted fleet structure is positioned to capture.

quote.text: War is God's way of teaching Americans geography — and, I would add, oil market dynamics.
